expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py create-range to define the Named Range
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py create-range ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Discount_Rate Assumptions B8' with JSON output: {"status": "ok", "name": "Inp_Discount_Rate", "reference": "Assumptions!B8"}

expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py write to set the value to 0.08
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'uv run scripts/idfa_ops.py write ops_test_model.xlsx Inp_Discount_Rate 0.08' with JSON output: {"status": "ok", "name": "Inp_Discount_Rate", "value": 0.08, "cell": "Assumptions!B8"}

expectations.text: Verifies the range was created correctly via read or inspect
expectations.evidence: Step 4 (Read): {"status": "ok", "values": {"Inp_Discount_Rate": 0.08}}. Step 5 (Inspect): shows count: 21 with Inp_Discount_Rate on Assumptions sheet, cell B8, value_raw: 0.08.

expectations.text: Uses the IDFA naming convention (Inp_ prefix)
expectations.evidence: Named Range is 'Inp_Discount_Rate' — uses the Inp_ prefix. Summary table confirms: 'Follows IDFA Inp_ convention: Yes'.
Methodology states: 'All operations performed via idfa_ops.py scripts (create-range, write, read, inspect). No manual cell editing. No formula evaluation required.'
